item.title: British American Tobacco brings back former executive Constantinescu as CFO
item.description: British American Tobacco on Thursday appointed Dragos Constantinescu ​as its chief financial officer, bringing ‌back a former executive who had spent 16 years at the tobacco giant before moving ​to Asahi Breweries.

item.title: China's March bank lending seen surging on post-holiday rebound
item.description: Chinese banks likely extended significantly more new loans in March than in February, according to a Reuters poll on Thursday, driven by improved credit demand ​and a seasonal rebound.

item.title: Morning Bid: There's a Gulf between market hopes and reality
item.description: A look at the day ahead in European and global markets from Wayne Cole.

item.title: South Korea's Kia cuts 2030 EV target over 20%, plans humanoid robots at U.S. factory
item.description: South Korea's Kia Corp said on Thursday that ​it aims to sell 1 ‌million electric vehicles (EVs) by 2030, lowering its earlier ​target by about 20% ​from 1.26 million announced last ⁠year, reflecting weaker ​EV demand and the impact ​of the U.S. scrapping EV subsidies last year.

item.title: OVHcloud launches new unit to meet demand from European militaries
item.description: France's OVHcloud is creating a ‌dedicated defence vertical after several European defence ministries approached it to support their military ​digital transformation, the datacentre operator said ​on Thursday.

item.title: Arm CEO Haas in line to lead much of parent SoftBank's international business, FT reports
item.description: Chip designer Arm's CEO Rene Haas is in line to lead ​much of parent SoftBank Group's international business ‌while keeping his current role at Arm, the Financial Times reported on Thursday, citing multiple people familiar with the ​matter.

item.title: Bessent urges Congress to pass crypto regulation bill
item.description: U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said Congress must pass a bill to create federal rules for digital assets, reiterating the ​push for rules that he says will ensure cryptocurrency development ‌and investment remain anchored in the U.S.

item.title: Foreign investors pour $18.65 billion into Japanese stocks on return after three weeks
item.description: Japanese stocks witnessed a huge influx of foreign funds in the week through April 4, a turnaround from ​three successive weeks of selling, with investor sentiment ‌stabilizing ahead of a ceasefire in the Iran war.

item.title: Goldman Sachs lowers second-quarter 2026 oil price forecasts
item.description: Goldman Sachs trimmed its second‑quarter 2026 forecasts for Brent and U.S. crude to $90 and $87 a barrel, respectively, ​late on Wednesday, after the U.S. and Iran agreed on ‌a two-week ceasefire.

item.title: BHP's incoming CEO met with Chinalco boss in China this week
item.description: BHP Group's incoming CEO, Brandon Craig, met with the ​chairman of Chinalco in Beijing this ‌week and discussed further cooperation, the Chinese aluminium giant said on Thursday.

item.title: Glencore charters supertanker to load oil from Middle East, sources say
item.description: Commodities trading firm Glencore has chartered a supertanker to load Middle Eastern crude for Asia, according to a shipping ​source and LSEG data, likely the first oil tanker to be ‌fixed for the route since the ceasefire in the U.S.-Iran war.

item.title: FedEx reaches tentative wage deal with pilots after years of talks
item.description: FedEx and a union representing more than 5,000 of its pilots ​reached a tentative deal on Wednesday, paving ‌the way for wages increases after years of negotiations.

item.title: Dollar steadies as fragile US-Iran ceasefire weighs on markets
item.description: The dollar caught its breath ​in early Thursday trade after broad losses, as investors anxiously assessed whether a fragile two-week ‌ceasefire between the United States and Iran would hold.

item.title: Asia stocks turn cautious as reality intrudes in Gulf
item.description: Asian share markets were in a more sober mood on ​Thursday as cracks quickly began to appear in the fragile Gulf ceasefire, nudging oil prices back up and reminding ‌investors the inflationary fallout will last for a long time yet.

item.title: Disney plans to cut 1,000 jobs, WSJ reports
item.description: Disney is planning to ​cut as many ‌as 1,000 positions in the ​coming weeks, ​many of which ⁠will be ​made in the ​company's marketing department, the Wall Street ​Journal reported ​on Wednesday, citing sources.

item.title: US crude futures rise after settling previous session with biggest fall in six years
item.description: U.S. crude futures rose on Thursday morning after settling ​with the biggest fall since ‌April 2020 during the previous trading session, after U.S. President Donald Trump ​agreed to a two-week ​ceasefire with Iran.

item.title: OpenAI will reserve portion of IPO shares for retail investors, CFO tells CNBC
item.description: OpenAI plans to reserve a portion of its shares from an initial public offering for individual investors, CFO Sarah Friar ​told CNBC on Wednesday, as the ChatGPT maker gears ‌up for a highly anticipated U.S. stock market listing.

item.title: US set to ease power limits on space-based broadband
item.description: The Federal Communications Commission is set to approve rule revisions this month to ease ​power limits on satellite spectrum use, giving a major boost to ‌space-based broadband services such as SpaceX's Starlink.

item.title: US court declines to block Pentagon's Anthropic blacklisting for now
item.description: A Washington, D.C., federal appeals court on Wednesday declined to block the Pentagon's national security blacklisting of Anthropic for now, a win for ​the Trump administration that comes after another appeals court came to the opposite conclusion ‌in a separate legal challenge by Anthropic.

item.title: Constellation Brands posts smaller‑than‑expected quarterly sales drop on steady Mexican beer demand
item.description: Corona beer maker Constellation Brands posted a smaller-than-expected drop in fourth-quarter sales on Wednesday, helped by stable demand for its Mexican lager ​brands such as Pacifico and Victoria.

item.title: Trading Day: Markets gorge on huge TACO
item.description: World markets soared on Wednesday and oil had its biggest fall in five years, as investors gave a hearty welcome to the ceasefire in the Iran war. The question now is whether this is just a mega-relief rally, or a major turning point in ​sentiment.

item.title: FAA proposes to fine American Airlines for alleged drug, alcohol violations
item.description: The U.S. Federal Aviation Administration ​said on Wednesday ‌it was proposing a $255,000 civil penalty against American ​Airlines for allegedly ​violating drug and alcohol ⁠regulations.

item.title: FCC to vote on proposal to ban Chinese labs from testing US electronics
item.description: The Federal Communications Commission said on Wednesday it will vote this month on ​a proposal to bar all Chinese labs from testing electronic devices such ‌as smartphones, cameras and computers for use in the U.S., widening a previous action targeting Beijing.

item.title: Southwest to fly wine for free in rare perk as airline fees rise
item.description: Southwest Airlines will fly wine for free from parts of the U.S. West Coast ​after launching new flights to California's wine ‌country, in a rare freebie as carriers pile on additional charges to cover high jet fuel costs.

item.title: Caterpillar elects Andrew Bonfield as new chief financial officer
item.description: Caterpillar ​said on ‌Wednesday it had appointed ​Andrew ​Bonfield as ⁠its ​chief ​financial officer, effective ​October ​1, 2026, ‌following ⁠eight years with ​the ​company.

item.title: Applied Digital beats quarterly revenue estimates on surging AI infrastructure demand
item.description: Applied Digital ​on Wednesday beat estimates for quarterly ‌revenue, driven by strong demand for its data center services from enterprises to support a boom ​in generative AI applications.

item.title: Fed rate cut bets revived, a bit, by Iran war ceasefire
item.description: Federal Reserve policymakers may ​consider cutting interest rates later this year now that an agreement for a two-week ceasefire in the ‌Iran conflict has eased concerns about a resurgence of inflation, but with uncertainty over the outlook for peace and oil prices still some 30% above their prewar levels, monetary policy easing is far from a done deal.

item.title: US banks could release $320 billion in capital with new draft rules, analysts say
item.description: Big U.S. banks may be able to release up to $320 billion in capital under revised draft rules unveiled by ​regulators last month, Morgan Stanley analysts estimated on Wednesday.

item.title: US pump prices to stay elevated despite US-Iran ceasefire deal, market observers say
item.description: U.S. consumers will continue to pay ​high prices to fill up their vehicles or purchase airplane tickets through the peak summer travel season, several market experts said on Wednesday, even as wholesale fuel prices ‌cooled after President Donald Trump announced a two-week ceasefire in the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran.

item.title: Fed's Daly says US economic fundamentals in a 'good place'
item.description: San Francisco Federal Reserve President ‌Mary Daly on Wednesday said the U.S. economy is fundamentally in a "good place" despite sharply higher oil prices from the Iran war and the uncertainty over how long the war will last.
